tasks.description: AI process analysis backend — API route (OpenRouter/DeepSeek), types, client wrapper, workspace settings caching, rate limiting
acceptance_criteria: New POST route at src/app/api/v1/ai/analyze-process/route.ts — accepts { workspace_id } in request body, authenticates user, validates workspace access via Supabase RLS

Route fetches all steps for the workspace via supabase.from('steps').select('*').eq('workspace_id', workspaceId) — gets maturity_score, target_maturity, effort_score, impact_score, executor, time_minutes, frequency_per_month, status, step_type, section_id
Route fetches all sections for the workspace via supabase.from('sections').select('*').eq('workspace_id', workspaceId) — for grouping context

Constructs a system prompt setting persona as process optimization consultant, and user prompt enumerating each section's steps with their actual metrics (name, status, executor, maturity current/target, time, frequency, effort, impact, gaps)

Calls OpenRouter API at https://openrouter.ai/api/v1/chat/completions using native fetch() — model: deepseek/deepseek-chat-v3-0324, temperature: 0.3, max_tokens: 4096, Authorization: Bearer ${process.env.OPENROUTER_API_KEY}
Parses response.choices[0].message.content as JSON into AIAnalysisResult type (with try/catch for parse failures)

Types added to src/types/database.ts: AIAnalysisResult { bottlenecks: AIInsight[], redundancies: AIInsight[], automation_candidates: AIInsight[], maturity_recommendations: AIInsight[] }, AIInsight { title: string, description: string, severity: 'high' | 'medium' | 'low', affected_step_ids: string[] }

Caches result by updating workspace settings JSONB directly via Supabase: merge { last_analysis: result, last_analysis_at: new Date().toISOString() } into existing settings object — preserves other settings keys
Rate limit: reads workspace.settings.last_analysis_at — if less than 5 minutes ago, returns 429 with { error: { code: 'rate_limited', message: '...' }, retry_after_seconds: N }

Error handling: OPENROUTER_API_KEY not set → returns 503 { error: { code: 'ai_not_configured', message: 'AI analysis requires an OpenRouter API key. Add OPENROUTER_API_KEY to your environment variables.' } }
Error handling: OpenRouter API failure → returns 502 with error detail from upstream
Client wrapper in src/lib/api/client.ts: analyzeProcess(workspaceId: string): Promise<AIAnalysisResult> using apiFetch<AIAnalysisResult>
Uses native fetch() only — zero new dependencies
logActivity() called on successful analysis (action: 'created', entity_type: 'ai_analysis')

similar_files: src/app/api/v1/improvement-ideas/route.ts — same auth + envelope + validation pattern
src/app/api/v1/workspaces/[id]/route.ts — workspace access pattern, settings column usage
patterns_to_follow: API envelope: successResponse(data) / errorResponse(code, message, status)
Auth guard: const { data: { user } } = await supabase.auth.getUser(); if (!user) return errorResponse('unauthorized', 'Not authenticated', 401);
apiFetch<T>() unwraps envelope, returns T directly — client wrapper returns apiFetch<AIAnalysisResult>(url, opts)
void logActivity() for fire-and-forget audit logging
Server-only env vars: process.env.OPENROUTER_API_KEY (no NEXT_PUBLIC_ prefix)
learnings_relevant: apiFetch unwraps envelope — client functions return apiFetch<T>() directly, no nested access
NEXT_PUBLIC_ static replacement — OPENROUTER_API_KEY is server-only, must NOT use NEXT_PUBLIC_ prefix
Supabase RLS + workspace access — use workspace fetch as implicit RLS check

tasks.special_instructions: OpenRouter uses OpenAI-compatible API format. Request body: { model: 'deepseek/deepseek-chat-v3-0324', messages: [{role:'system', content: systemPrompt}, {role:'user', content: userPrompt}], temperature: 0.3, max_tokens: 4096, response_format: { type: 'json_object' } }. Response shape: { choices: [{ message: { content: string } }] } — content is a JSON string to parse. The workspace settings JSONB column already exists (migration 003, DEFAULT '{}'). To cache: fetch current workspace, spread existing settings, add last_analysis and last_analysis_at, then update. Use direct Supabase update: supabase.from('workspaces').update({ settings: { ...currentSettings, last_analysis: result, last_analysis_at: isoDate } }).eq('id', workspaceId). Do NOT modify the workspace PATCH API route. Step data: steps table has workspace_id directly — query supabase.from('steps').select('*').eq('workspace_id', workspaceId). Sections table also has workspace_id. Prompt structure: system prompt sets AI as 'senior process optimization consultant analyzing a business process map', user prompt lists each section with its steps and their metrics. Request JSON output matching AIAnalysisResult schema. If workspace has no steps, return empty analysis (no API call needed). The route file goes in src/app/api/v1/ai/analyze-process/route.ts — create the ai/ and analyze-process/ directories.

tasks.description: Add delete action button on improvement idea cards with confirmation
acceptance_criteria: Each improvement card has a Trash2 icon button in the top-right area (next to priority badge)
Click triggers window.confirm('Delete this improvement idea?') — simple pattern matching other delete actions
On confirm, calls deleteImprovementIdea(idea.id) imported from @/lib/api/client
On success, removes idea from local state: setIdeas(prev => prev.filter(i => i.id !== idea.id))
On error, calls toastError('Failed to delete improvement', { error: err })
Delete button styled subtle: text-[var(--text-tertiary)] hover:text-[#EF4444] transition-colors, Trash2 h-3.5 w-3.5
TypeScript compiles without errors

patterns_to_follow: Import Trash2 from lucide-react (add to existing import line 5)
toastError pattern already used in handleStatusChange (line 63)

tasks.special_instructions: deleteImprovementIdea already exists in client.ts — do NOT modify client.ts. Only modify improvements-view.tsx. Add the delete button inside each card's top-right badge area. Add a handleDelete async function similar to handleStatusChange. Use window.confirm() for simplicity (this is a lightweight improvement, not a major feature). The function should also be async with try/catch wrapping the API call.

tasks.description: Prioritization 'Go to Canvas' link targets first process-type tab instead of tabs[0]
acceptance_criteria: Go to Canvas link targets first tab with canvas_type === 'process' instead of tabs[0]
Falls back to tabs[0] if no process-type tab exists
Link still hidden when tabs.length === 0
TypeScript compiles without errors

patterns_to_follow: Replace tabs[0].id at line 169 with (tabs.find(t => t.canvas_type === 'process') ?? tabs[0]).id

tasks.special_instructions: Minimal one-line change at line 169 of prioritization-view.tsx. Change `tabs[0].id` to `(tabs.find(t => t.canvas_type === 'process') ?? tabs[0]).id`. The Tab type from database.ts includes canvas_type. Verify the tabs prop passed to PrioritizationView includes canvas_type in its type signature — if using a subset type, ensure canvas_type is included.

shared_files.note: src/types/database.ts and src/lib/api/client.ts owned exclusively by slot 1. No other slot may modify these files.
